author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, John i. 1, ‘In the beginning was the word, and the word was with God, and the word was God,’ etc. Against the Arians.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, John i. 1, ‘In the beginning was the word, and the word was with God, and the word was God,’ etc. Against the Arians.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The section of the Gospel which has been read, most dearly beloved brethren, looketh for the pure eye of the heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For from John’s Gospel we have understood our Lord Jesus Christ according to His Divinity for the creating of the whole creation, and according to His Humanity for the recovery of the creature fallen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now in this same Gospel we find what sort and how great a man was John, that from the dignity of the dispenser it may be understood of how great a price is the Word which could be announced by such a man; yea, rather how without price is That which surpasseth all things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For any purchasable thing is either equal to the price, or it is below it, or it exceeds it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When any one procures a thing for as much as it is worth, the price is equal to the thing which is procured; when for less, it is below it; when for more, it exceeds it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But to the Word of God nothing can either be equalled, or to exchange can anything be below It, or above It.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For all things can be below the Word of God, for that “all things were made by Him;” yet are they not in such wise below, as if they were the price of the Word, that any one should give something to receive That.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet if we may say so, and if any principle or custom of speaking admit this expression, the price for procuring the Word, is the procurer himself, who will have given himself for himself to This Word.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Accordingly when we buy anything we look out for something to give, that for the price we give we may have the thing we wish to buy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And that which we give is without us; and if it was with us before, what we give becomes without us, that that which we procure may be with us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whatever price the purchaser may find it, it must needs be such as that he gives what he has, and receives what he has not; yet so that he from whom the price goes himself remains, and that for which he gives the price is added to him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But whoso would procure this Word, whoso would have it, let him not seek for anything without himself to give, let him give himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when he shall have done this, he doth not lose himself, as he loseth the price when he buys anything.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Word of God then is set forth before all men; let them who can, procure It, and they can who have a godly will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in That Word is peace; and “peace on earth is to men of good will.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then whoso will procure it, let him give himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is as it were the price of the Word, if so it may in any way be said, when he that giveth doth not lose himself, and gaineth the Word for which he giveth himself, and gaineth himself too in the Word to whom he giveth himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what giveth he to the Word?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not ought that is any other’s than His, for whom he giveth himself; but what by the Same Word was made, that is given back to Him to be remade; “All things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If all things, then of course man too.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the heaven, and earth, and sea, and all things that are therein, if the whole creation; of course more manifestly he, who being made after the image of God by the Word was made man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am not now, brethren, discussing how the words, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God,” can be understood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: After an ineffable sort it may be understood; it cannot by the words of man he made to be understood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am treating of the Word of God, and telling you why It is not understood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am not now speaking to make It understood, but I tell you what hinders It from being understood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He is a certain Form, a Form not formed, but the Form of all things formed; a Form unchangeable, without failure, without decay, without thee, without place, surpassing all things, being in all things, as at once a kind of foundation in which they are, and a Head-stone under which they are.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If you say that all things are in Him, you lie not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For This Word is called the Wisdom of God; and we have it written, “In Wisdom hast Thou made all things.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, then in Him are all things: and yet in that He is God, under Him are all things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am showing how incomprehensible is what has been read; yet it has been read, not that it should be comprehended by man, but that man should sorrow that he comprehends it not, and find out whereby he is hindered from comprehending, and remove those hindrances, and, himself changed from worse to better, aspire after the perception of the unchangeable Word.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Word doth not advance or increase by the addition of those who know It; but is Entire, if thou abide; Entire, if thou depart; Entire, when thou dost return; abiding in Itself, and renewing all things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is then the Form of all things, the Form unfashioned, without thee, as I have said, and without space.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For whatsoever is contained in space, is circumscribed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Every form is circumscribed by bounds; it hath limits wherefrom and whereunto it reaches.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, what is contained in place, and has extension in a sort of bulk and space, is less in its parts than in the whole.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God grant that ye may understand.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now from the bodies which are day by day before our eyes, which we see, which we touch, among which we live, we are able to judge how that every body hath a form in space.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now everything which occupies a certain space, is less in its parts than in its whole.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The arm, for instance, is a part of the human body; of course the arm is less than the whole body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And if the arm be less, it occupies a smaller space.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So again the head, in that it is a part of the body, is contained in less space, and is less than the whole body of which it is the head.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So all things which are in space, are less in their several parts than in the whole.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us entertain no such idea, no such thought concerning That Word.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us not form our conceptions of spiritual things from the suggestion of the flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That Word, That God, is not less in part than in the whole.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But thou art not able to conceive of any such thing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Such ignorance is more pious than presumptuous knowledge.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we are speaking of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is said, “And the Word was God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We are speaking of God; what marvel, if thou do not comprehend?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if thou comprehend, He is not God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be there a pious confession of ignorance, rather than a rash profession of knowledge.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To reach to God in any measure by the mind, is a great blessedness; but to comprehend Him, is altogether impossible.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God is an object for the mind, He is to be understood; a body is for the eyes, it is to be seen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But thinkest thou that thou comprehendest a body by the eye?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou canst not at all.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For whatever thou lookest at, thou dost not see the whole.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou seest a man’s face, thou dost not see his back at the time thou seest the face; and when thou seest the back, thou dost not at that time see the face.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou dost not then so see, as to comprehend; but when thou seest another part which thou hadst not seen before, unless memory aid thee to remember that thou hast seen that from which thou dost withdraw, thou couldest never say that thou hadst comprehended anything even on the surface.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou handlest what thou seest, turnest it about on this side and that, or thyself dost go round it to see the whole.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In one view then thou canst not see the whole.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And as long as thou turnest it about to see it, thou art but seeing the parts; and by putting together that thou hast seen the other parts, thou dost fancy that thou seest the whole.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But this must not be understood as the sight of the eyes, but the activity of the memory.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then can be said, Brethren, of that Word?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, of the bodies which are before our eyes we say they cannot comprehend them by a glance; what eye of the heart then comprehendeth God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Enough that it reach to Him if the eye be pure.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if it reach, it reacheth by a sort of incorporeal and spiritual touch, yet it doth not comprehend; and that, only if it be pure.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And a man is made blessed by touching with the heart That which ever abideth Blessed; and that is this Very Everlasting Blessedness, and that Everlasting Life, whereby man is made to live; that Perfect Wisdom, whereby man is made wise; that Everlasting Light, whereby man becomes enlightened.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And see how by this touch thou art made what thou wast not, thou dost not make that thou touchest be what it was not before.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I repeat it, there grows no increase to God from them that know Him, but to them that know Him, from the knowledge of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us not suppose, dearly beloved Brethren, that we confer any benefit on God, because I have said that we give Him in a manner a price.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we do not give Him aught whereby He can be increased, Who when thou fallest away, is Entire, and when thou returnest, abideth Entire, ready to make Himself seen that He may bless those who turn to Him, and punish those with blindness who turn away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For by this blindness, as the beginning of punishment, doth He first execute vengeance on the soul that turns away from Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For whoso turns away from the True Light, that is from God, is at once made blind.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is not yet sensible of his punishment, but he hath it already.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Accordingly, dearly beloved brethren, let us understand that the Word of God is incorporeally, inviolably, unchangeably, without temporal nativity, yet born of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do we think that we can any how persuade certain unbelievers that that is not it, consistent with the truth, which is said by us according to the Catholic faith, which is contrary to the Arians, by whom the Church of God hath been often tried, forasmuch as carnal men receive with greater ease what they have been accustomed to see?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For some have dared to say, “The Father is greater than the Son, and precedes Him in thee;” that is, the Father is greater than the Son, and the Son is less than the Father, and is preceded by the Father in thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And they argue thus; “If He was born, of course the Father was before His Son was born to Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend; may He be with me, whilst your prayers assist me, and with godly heed desire to receive what He may give, what He may suggest to me; may He be with me, that I may be able in some sort to explain what I have begun.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet, brethren, I tell you before I begin, if I shall not be able to explain it, do not suppose that it is the failure of the proof, but of the man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Accordingly I exhort and entreat you to pray; that the mercy of God may be with me, and make the matter be so explained by me, as is meet for you to hear, and for me to speak.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They then say thus; “If He be the Son of God, He was born.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This we confess.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He would not be a Son, if He were not born.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is plain, the faith admits it, the Catholic Church approves it, it is truth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They then go on; “If the Son was born to the Father, the Father was before the Son was born to Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This the faith rejects, Catholic ears reject it, it is anathematized, whoso entertains this conceit is without, he belongs not to the fellowship and society of the saints.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then says he, “Give me an explanation, how the Son could be born to the Father, and yet be coeval with Him of whom He was born?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what can we do, brethren, when we are conveying lessons of spiritual things to carnal men; even if so be we ourselves too are not carnal, when we intimate these spiritual truths to carnal men, to men accustomed to the idea of earthly nativities, and seeing the order of these creatures, where succession and departure separates off in age them that beget and them that are begotten?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For after the father the son is born, to succeed the father, who in time of course must die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This do we find in men, this in other animals, that the parents are first, the children after them in time.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Through this custom of observation they desire to transfer carnal things to spiritual, and by their intentness on carnal things are more easily led into error.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is not the reason of the hearers which follows those who preach such things, but custom which even entangles themselves, that they do preach such things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what shall we do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Shall we keep silence?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Would that we might!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For perchance by silence something might be thought of worthy of the unspeakable subject.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For whatsoever cannot be spoken, is unspeakable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now God is unspeakable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if the Apostle Paul saith, that he “was caught up even unto the third heaven, and that he heard unspeakable words;” how much more unspeakable is He, who showed such things, which could not be spoken by him to whom they were shown?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then, brethren, it were better if we could keep silence, and say, “This the faith contains; so we believe; thou art not able to receive it, thou art but a babe; thou must patiently endure till thy wings be grown, lest when thou wouldest fly without wings, it should not be the free course of liberty, but the fall of temerity.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What do they say against this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “O if he had anything to say, he would say it to me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is the mere excuse of one who is at fault.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is overcome by the truth, who does not choose to answer.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He to whom this is said, if he make no answer, though he be not conquered in himself is yet conquered in the wavering brethren.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the weak brethren hear it, and they think that there is really nothing to be said; and perhaps they think right that there is nothing to be said, yet not that there is nothing to be felt.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For a man can express nothing which he cannot also feel; but he may feel something which he cannot express.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nevertheless, saving the unspeakableness of that Sovereign Majesty, lest when we shall have produced certain similitudes against them, any one should think that we have by them arrived at that which cannot be expressed or conceived by babes (and if it can be at all even by the more advanced, it can only be in part, only in a riddle, only “through a glass;” but not as yet, “face to face”), let us too produce certain similitudes against them, whereby they may be refuted, not “it” comprehended.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when we say that it may very possibly happen, that it may be understood, that He may both be born, and yet Coeternal with Him of whom He was born, in order to refute this, and prove it as it were to be false, they bring forth similitudes against us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From whence?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From the creatures, and they say to us, “Every man of course was before he begat a son, he is greater in age than his son; and so a horse was before he begat his foal, and a sheep, and the other animals.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus do they bring similitudes from the creatures.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What! must we labour too, that we may find resemblances of those things which we are establishing?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what if I should not find any, might I not rightly say, “The Nativity of the Creator hath, it may be, no resemblance of itself among the creatures?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as far as He surpasseth the things which are here, in that He is there, so far doth He surpass the things which are born here, in that He was born there.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All things here have their being from God; and yet what is to be compared with God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So all things which are born here, are born by His agency.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so perhaps there is no resemblance of His Nativity found, as there is none found whether of His Substance, Unchangeableness, Divinity, Majesty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what can be found here like these?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then it chance that no resemblance of His Nativity either be found, am I therefore overwhelmed, because I have not found resemblances to the Creator of all things, when desiring to find in the creature what is like the Creator?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And in very truth, Brethren, I am not likely to discover any temporal resemblances which I can compare to eternity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But as to those which thou hast discovered, what are they?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What hast thou discovered?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That a father is greater in time than his son; and therefore thou wouldest have the Son of God to be less in time than the Eternal Father, because thou hast found that a son is less than a father born in time.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Find me an eternal father here, and thou hast found a resemblance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou findest a son less than a father in time, a temporal son less than a temporal father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hast thou found me a temporal son younger than eternal father?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Seeing then that in Eternity is stability, but in time variety; in Eternity all things stand still, in time one thing comes, another succeeds; thou canst find a son of lesser age succeeding his father in the variety of time, for that he himself succeeded to his father also, not a son born in time to a father eternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How then, Brethren, can we find in the creature aught coeternal, when in the creature we find nothing eternal?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do thou find an eternal father in the creature, and I will find a coeternal son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if thou find not an eternal father, and the one surpasses the other in thee; it is sufficient, that for a resemblance I find something coeval.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what is coeternal is one thing what is coeval another.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Every day we call them coeval who have the same measure of times; the one is not preceded by the other in time, yet they both whom we call coeval once began to “be.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now if I shall be able to discover something which is born coeval with that of which it is born; if two coeval things can be discovered, that which begets, and that which is begotten; we discover in this case things coeval, let us understand in the other things coeternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If here I shall find that a thing begotten hath begun to be ever since that which begets began to be, we may understand at least that the Son of God did not begin to be, ever since He that begat Him did not begin to be.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, brethren, perhaps we may discover something in the creature, which is born of something else, and which yet began to be at the same time as that of which it is born began to be.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the latter case, the one began to be when the other began to be; in the former the one did not begin to be, ever since the other began not to be.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The first then is coeval, the second coeternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I suppose that your holiness has understood already what I am saying, that temporal things cannot be compared to eternal; but that by some slight and small resemblance, things coeval may be with things coeternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us find accordingly two coeval things; and let us get our hints as to these resemblances from the Scriptures.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We read in the Scriptures of Wisdom, “For she is the Brightness of the Everlasting Light.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again we read, “The unspotted Mirror of the Majesty of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wisdom Herself is called, “The Brightness of the Everlasting Light,” is called, “The Image of the Father;” from hence let us take a resemblance, that we may find two coeval things, from which we may understand things coeternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O thou Arian, if I shall find that something that begets does not precede in time that which it begat, that a thing begotten is not less in time than that of which it is begotten; it is but just that thou concede to me, that these coeternals may be found in the Creator, when coevals can be found in the creature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I think that this indeed occurs already to some brethren.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For some anticipated me as soon as I said, “For She is the Brightness of the Everlasting Light.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the fire throws out light, light is thrown out from the fire.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If we ask which comes from which, every day when we light a candle are we reminded of some invisible and indescribable thing, that the candle as it were of our understanding may be lighted in this night of the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Observe him who lights a candle.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: While the candle is not lighted, there is as yet no fire, nor any brightness which proceedeth from the fire.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But I ask, saying, “Does the brightness come from the fire, or the fire from the brightness?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Every soul answers me (for it has pleased God to sow the beginnings of understanding and wisdom in every soul); every soul answers me, and no one doubts, that that brightness comes from the fire, not the fire from the brightness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then look at the fire as the father of that brightness; for I have said before that we are looking for things coeval, not coeternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If I desire to light a candle, there is as yet no fire there, nor yet that brightness; but immediately that I have lighted it, together with the fire comes forth the brightness also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give me then here a fire without brightness, and I believe you that the Father ever was without the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend; The matter has been explained by me as so great a matter could be, by the Lord helping the earnestness of your prayers, and the preparation of your heart, ye have taken in as much as ye were able to receive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet these things are ineffable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not suppose that anything worthy of the subject has been spoken, if it only be for that things carnal are compared with coeternal, things temporal with things abiding ever, things subject to extinction to things immortal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But inasmuch as the Son is said also to be the Image of the Father, let us take from this too a sort of resemblance, though in things very different, as I have said before.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The image of a man looking into a glass is thrown out from the glass.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But this cannot assist us for the clearing of that which we are endeavouring in some sort to explain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is said to me, “A man who looks into a glass of course, ‘was’ already, and was born before that.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The image came out only as soon as he looked at himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For a man who looks in a glass, ‘was’ before he came to the glass.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then shall we find, from which we may be able to draw out such a resemblance, as we did from the fire and the brightness?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us find one from a very little thing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You know without any difficulty how water often throws out the images of bodies.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I mean, when any one is passing, or standing still along the water, he sees his own image there.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us suppose then something born on the water’s side, as a shrub, or an herb, is it not born together with its image?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As soon as ever it begins to be, its image begins to be with it, it does not precede in its birth its own image; it cannot be showed to me that anything is born upon the water’s side, and that its image has appeared afterwards, whereas it first appeared without its image; but it is born together with its image; and yet the image comes from it, not it from the image.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is born then together with its image, and the shrub and its image begin to be together.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dost thou not confess that the image is begotten of that shrub, not the shrub of the image?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then thou dost confess that the image is from that shrub.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Accordingly that which begets and that which is begotten began to “be” together.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore they are coeval.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the shrub had been always, the image from the shrub would have been always too.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now that which has its being from something else, is of course born of it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is possible then that one that begets might always be, and always be together with that which was born of him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For here it was that we were in perplexity and trouble, how the Eternal Nativity might be understood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then the Son of God is so called on this principle, that there is the Father also, that He hath One from whom He derives His Being; not on this, that the Father is first in time, and the Son after.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father always was, the Son always from the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And because whatever “is” from another thing, is born, therefore the Son was always born.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father always was, the image from Him always was; as that image of the shrub was born of the shrub, and if the shrub had always been, the image would also have always been born from the shrub.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou couldest not find things begotten coeternal with the eternal begetters, but thou hast found things born coeval with those that begat them in time.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I understand the Son coeternal with the Eternal who begat Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what with regard to things of time is coeval, with regard to things eternal is coeternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here there is somewhat for you to consider, Brethren, as a protection against blasphemies.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is constantly said, “See thou hast produced certain resemblances; but the brightness which is thrown out from the fire, shines less brilliantly than the fire itself, and the image of the shrub has less proper subsistence, than that shrub of which it is the image.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These instances have a resemblance, but they have not a thorough equality: wherefore they do not seem to be of the same substance.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then shall we say, if any one say, “The Father then is to the Son, such as the brightness is to the fire, and the image to the shrub”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See I have understood the Father to be eternal; and the Son to be coeternal with Him; nevertheless say we that He is as the brightness which is thrown out from and is less brilliant than the fire, or as the image which is reflected from and has less real existence than the shrub?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No, but there is a thorough equality.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I do not believe it,” he will say, “because thou hast not discovered a resemblance.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Well then, believe the Apostle, because he was able to see what I have said.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he says, “He thought it not robbery to be equal with God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Equality is perfect likeness in every way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what said he?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Not robbery.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because that is robbery which belongs to another.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet from these two comparisons, these two kinds, we may perhaps find in the creature a resemblance whereby we may understand how the Son is both coeternal with the Father, and in no respect less than He.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But this we cannot find in one kind of resemblances singly: let us join both kinds together.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How both kinds?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: One, of which they themselves give instances of resemblances, and the other, of which we gave.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they gave instances of resemblances from those things which are born in time, and are preceded in time by them of whom they are born, as man of man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He that is born first is greater in time; but yet man and man, that is of the same substance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For man begets a man, and a horse a horse, and a sheep a sheep.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These beget after the same substance, but not after the same time.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are diverse in time, but not in nature diverse.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then do we praise here in this nativity?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The equality of nature surely.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what is wanting?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The equality of time.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us retain the one thing which is praised here, that is, the equality of nature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But in the other kind of resemblances, which we gave from the brightness of the fire and the image of the shrub, you find not an equality of nature, you do find an equality of time.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What do we praise here?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Equality of time.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is wanting?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Equality of nature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Join the things which you praise together.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in the creatures there is wanting something which you praise, in the Creator nothing can be wanting: because what you find in the creature, came forth from the Hand of the Creator.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is there in things coeval?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Must not that be given to God which you praise here in?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what is wanting must not be attributed to that Sovereign Majesty, in the which there is no defect.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See I offer to you things begetting coeval with things begotten: in these you praise the equality of time, but find fault with the inequality of nature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What you find fault with, do not attribute to God; what you praise, attribute to Him; so from this kind of resemblances you attribute to Him instead of a cotemporaneousness a coeternity, that the Son may be coeternal with Him of whom He was born.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But from the other kind of resemblances, which itself too is a creature of God, and ought to praise the Creator, what do you praise in them?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Equality of nature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You had before assigned coeternity by reason of the first distinction; by reason of this last, assign equality; and the nativity of the same substance is complete.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what is more mad, my brethren, than that I should praise the creature in anything which does not exist in the Creator?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In man I praise equality of nature, shall I not believe it in Him who made man?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That which is born of man is man; shall not that which is born of God, be That which He is of whom He was born?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Converse have I none with works which God hath not made.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let then all the works of the Creator praise Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I find in the one case a cotemporaneousness, I get at the knowledge of a coeternity in the other.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the first I find an equality of nature, I understand an equality of substance in the other.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this then that is “wholly,” which in the other case is found in the several parts, and several things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is then “wholly” here altogether, and not only what is in the creature; I find it wholly here, but as being in the Creator, in so much higher a way, in that the one is visible, the Other Invisible; the one temporal, the Other Eternal; the one changeable, the Other Unchangeable; the one corruptible, the Other Incorruptible.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lastly, in the case of men themselves, what we find, man and man, are two men; here the Father and the Son are One God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I render unspeakable thanks to our Lord God, that He hath vouchsafed, at your prayers, to deliver my infirmity from this most perplexed and difficult place.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet above all things remember this, that the Creator transcends indescribably whatever we could gather from the creature, whether by the bodily senses, or the thought of the mind.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But wouldest thou with the mind reach Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Purify thy mind, purify thine heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Make clean the eye whereby That, whatever It be, may be reached.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For “blessed are the clean in heart, for they shall see God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But whilst the heart was not cleansed, what could be provided and granted more mercifully by Him, than that That Word of whom we have spoken so great and so many things, and yet have spoken nothing worthy of Him; that That Word, “by whom all things were made,” should become that which we are, that we might be able to attain to That which we are not?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we are not God; but with the mind or the interior eye of the heart we can see God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our eyes dulled by sins, blinded, enfeebled by infirmity, desire to see; but we are in hope, not yet in possession.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We are the children of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This saith John, who says, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God;” he who lay on the Lord’s Breast, who drew in these secrets from the Bosom of His Heart; he says, “Dearly beloved, we are the children of God, and it doth not yet appear what we shall be; we know that, when He shall appear, we shall be like Him, for we shall see Him as He is.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is promised us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But in order that we may attain, if we cannot yet see God the Word, let us hear the Word made Flesh; seeing we are carnal, let us hear the Word Incarnate.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For for this cause came He, for this cause took upon Him our infirmity, that thou mightest be able to receive the strong words of a God bearing thy weakness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And He is truly called “milk.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He giveth milk to infants, that He may give the meat of wisdom to them of riper years.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Suck then now with patience, that thou mayest be fed to thy heart’s most eager wish.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For how is even the milk, wherewith infants are suckled, made?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Was it not solid meat on the table?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the infant is not strong enough to eat the meat which is on the table; what does the mother do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She turns the meat into the substance of her flesh, and makes milk of it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Makes for us what we may be able to take.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So the Word was made Flesh, that we little ones, who were indeed as infants with respect to food, might be nourished by milk.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But there is this difference; that when the mother makes the food turned into flesh milk, the food is turned into milk; whereas the Word abiding Itself unchangeably assumed Flesh, that there might be, as it were, a tissue of the two.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What He is, He did not corrupt or change, that in thy fashion, He might speak to thee, not transformed and turned into man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For abiding unalterable, unchangeable, and altogether inviolable, He became what thou art in respect of thee, what He is in Himself in respect of the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what doth He say Himself to the infirm, to the end that recovering that sight, they may be able in some measure to reach the Word by whom all things were made?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Come unto Me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will refresh you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Take My yoke upon you, and learn of Me, that I am meek and lowly in heart.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What doth the Master, the Son of God, the Wisdom of God, by whom all things were made, proclaim?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He calleth the human race, and saith, “Come unto Me, all ye that labour, and learn of Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wast thinking haply that the Wisdom of God would say, “Learn how I have made the heavens and the stars; how all things also were numbered in Me before they were made, how by virtue of unchangeable principles your very hairs were numbered.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Didst thou think that Wisdom would say these things, and such as these?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No. But first that.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That I am meek and lowly in heart.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, see here what ye can comprehend, brethren; it is surely a little thing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We are making our way to great things, let us receive the little things, and we shall be great.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wouldest thou comprehend the height of God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: First comprehend the lowliness of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Condescend to be humble for thine own sake, seeing that God condescended to be humble for thy sake too; for it was not for His own.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Comprehend then the lowliness of Christ, learn to be humble, be loth to be proud.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Confess thine infirmity, lie patiently before the Physician; when thou shalt have comprehended His lowliness, thou risest with Him; not as though He should rise Himself in that He is the Word; but thou rather, that He may be more and more comprehended by thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: At first thou didst understand falteringly and hesitatingly; afterwards thou wilt understand more surely and more clearly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He doth not increase, but thou makest progress, and He seemeth as it were to rise with thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So it is, brethren.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Believe the commandments of God, and do them, and He will give you the strength of understanding.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not put the last first, and, as it were, prefer knowledge to the commandments of God; lest ye be only the lower, and none the more firmly rooted.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider a tree; first it strikes downwards, that it may grow up on high; fixes its root low in the ground, that it may extend its top to heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Does it make an effort to grow except from humiliation?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And wouldest thou without charity comprehend these transcendent matters, shoot toward the heaven without a root?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This were a ruin, not a growing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With “Christ” then “dwelling in your hearts by faith, be ye rooted and grounded in charity, that ye may be filled with all the fulness of God.”
